items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has sanctioned the Scheme of Amalgamation of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) with TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra). The Scheme has been sanctioned in exercise of the powers conferred under sub-section (4) of Section 44A read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. The Scheme will come into force with effect from May 04, 2026 (Monday). The branches of The Bhavani Sahakari Bank Ltd., Mumbai (Maharashtra) will function as branches of TJSB Sahakari Bank Ltd. (Maharashtra) with effect from May 04, 2026. (Brij Raj) Chief General Manage

items.title: Fuel price hikes, West Asia tensions push Indian businesses towards EV transition
items.summary: Rising crude oil prices are pushing India towards electric vehicles. Businesses are seeking stable operational costs. This shift is driven by economic necessity. Geopolitical instability in West Asia adds to concerns about fossil fuel dependence. India's EV adoption looks strong, driven by policy and cost savings. Fleet operators prefer local EV solutions.

items.title: Can Nifty 50 hit 30,000 by FY27-end? Smallcase managers remain bullish despite 9% decline YTD
items.summary: Despite a 9% decline in Nifty this year, smallcase managers are optimistic about FY27, predicting the index will reach 28,000–30,000. They emphasize earnings growth over valuation expansion, highlighting sectors like Banking and Capital Goods for future gains.

items.title: S&P 500 is set for longest weekly rally since 2023: Markets Wrap
items.summary: Two-year yields hit the highest since February 2025 as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller supports making clear the next rate move is just as likely to be a raise as a cut

items.title: Oil swings with market focused on US-Iran peace prospects
items.summary: The war and the curtailment in supplies have seen global stockpiles of crude oil and products being drawn down at a record pace, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc

items.title: Multibagger Opportunity? REITs may be the next big wealth creation avenue for India’s retail investors
items.summary: India's Real Estate Investment Trusts are growing into a popular investment for everyday people. These trusts are moving beyond just office buildings to include warehouses, hotels, and shopping centers. Experts see this as a major step for the market. REITs offer stable income and a way to invest in large properties without direct ownership.

items.title: EisnerAmper India elevates Karthick Venkatakrishnan as managing director
items.summary: EisnerAmper India has appointed Karthick Venkatakrishnan as managing director to spearhead growth and sales strategy across India and the APAC region. In his new role, Venkatakrishnan will focus on expanding the firm's brand presence in advisory, tax, and fund administration services, identifying new opportunities and showcasing its unique capabilities to clients throughout the area.

items.title: LRS outflows up over 10% in March led by travel demand
items.summary: Indians sent $2.59 billion abroad in March 2026 via the Liberalised Remittance Scheme. This marked a 10.9% increase from the previous month. Travel spending saw a seasonal dip. However, investments in equity and debt surged significantly. Deposits abroad also showed a strong recovery. Remittances for education moderated after a peak in January.

items.title: Former Dentons Link Legal partner Kshama Loya launches boutique arbitration chambers
items.summary: Kshama A Loya has launched a specialist arbitration practice in Mumbai. She previously worked as a partner at Dentons Link Legal. Loya will focus on domestic and international commercial arbitration. Her expertise includes investment treaty disputes and enforcement of arbitral awards.

items.title: Supply-side stress, weather add uncertainty to macros
items.summary: India's economy faces near-term challenges. Supply issues and weather create uncertainty. Geopolitical events in West Asia could impact domestic prices. Despite these concerns, domestic demand remains strong. The Reserve Bank of India is monitoring inflation closely. Robust exports and policy measures offer support against external risks.

items.title: India Rupee shows strong rebound on firm RBI support
items.summary: On Friday, the Indian rupee made a notable leap, finishing the day above the 96 per dollar threshold. The surge was largely attributed to robust actions from the Reserve Bank of India, following a dip towards 97 mid-week. Traders noted a wave of dollar offerings from state-run banks, part of a broader strategy to stabilize the rupee's performance.

items.title: Cockroach Janta Party: How an insect jibe united India’s disgruntled jobless youth
items.summary: A mock political party, the Cockroach Janta Party, is gaining massive followers on social media. It represents young Indians' frustration with job scarcity and traditional politics. The movement highlights growing Gen Z resentment towards the government's employment efforts. This satirical party has captured the attention of political rivals as well.

items.title: Stocks to buy under ₹200: Mehul Kothari of Anand Rathi recommends three shares to buy or sell
items.summary: Kothari recommends Yes Bank, Punjab National Bank, and Bandhan Bank for short-term investments under ₹200. The market remains cautious amid global uncertainties and rising crude oil prices.

items.title: Patient capital trusts India as hot money exits; FDI inflow rises 17% YoY
items.summary: In the fiscal year 2025-26, India witnessed a remarkable surge in foreign direct investment, soaring to $94.5 billion. This uptick is particularly notable given the backdrop of global economic turbulence. Investors from abroad demonstrated a keen interest, predominantly in the finance and technology arenas. Additionally, net FDI inflows experienced a noteworthy rise, underscoring India’s growing attractiveness to international financiers.

items.title: D-Street ends week in the green with war and peace on its mind
items.summary: Friday brought a wave of optimism to Indian stock markets, with notable weekly advancements. Nonetheless, investors remained watchful as talks between the US and Iran unfolded. The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex ended the day higher, driven largely by gains in the banking and financial sectors.

items.title: Retail players can skip Central Bank of India OFS, say analysts, cite margin and earnings concerns
items.summary: Retail investors are being urged by market analysts to steer clear of the government's shares sale in Central Bank of India. The bank's current margin issues and uncertain future profitability raise red flags. Despite the attractive discount on the offer price, experts forecast further share sales needed for compliance, leading to persistent sales pressure on the stock.

items.title: Bond slump stirs vigilante fears
items.summary: A sense is setting in among investment professionals that we have a serious problem here

items.title: Get ready to take sides in Big Tech’s giant IPO bake-off
items.summary: With SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic targeting the same market, investors must decide what qualities they prize most

items.title: Superdrug owner shrugs off volatility to press ahead with $30bn dual listing
items.summary: AS Watson plans to publicly trade shares in both Hong Kong and London before the end of 2026

items.title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked by 87-91 paise per litre for third time in eight days
Wall Street Week Ahead: Soaring stocks could face rocky patch as earnings wind down, yields perk up
items.summary: US stocks face potential headwinds as a strong earnings season concludes. Investors are shifting focus to rising inflation and bond yields, with the PCE price index due Thursday. Futures now price in a potential Fed rate hike later in 2026, a stark contrast to earlier expectations. Key reports from Salesforce and Costco are awaited.

items.title: Buy or sell: Sumeet Bagadia recommends three stocks to buy on Monday - 25 May 2026
items.summary: Sumeet Bagadia suggests buying Wipro and Eicher Motors due to bullish signals and recovery from declines. Wipro is recommended at ₹200-203 with a target of ₹213, while Eicher Motors is advised at ₹6,980 targeting ₹7,200-7,300.

items.title: HDFC Bank backs Hyderabad luxury housing project with Rs 300 crore
items.summary: HDFC Bank has provided Rs 300 crore in funding for a Rs 2,000 crore ultra-luxury residential project in Hyderabad's Kokapet. This investment highlights a trend of developers seeking structured financing amid moderating sales and strong demand for premium homes, with Hyderabad leading southern India's luxury market.

items.title: Marco Rubio's trip to 'great partner' India signals US need to repair ties
items.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is visiting India to strengthen a partnership strained by tariffs and renewed U.S. engagement with Pakistan and China. The trip aims to boost trade, energy, and defense cooperation, addressing concerns over trade disputes and India's reliance on Russian oil. Despite efforts to stabilize ties, a comprehensive trade agreement remains elusive.

items.title: CNG prices hiked for third time in 10 days, gets costlier by Rs 1 per kg
items.summary: CNG prices in Delhi have seen another Re 1 hike, the third in ten days, following similar increases in petrol and diesel. This surge is attributed to global energy market pressures stemming from Middle East tensions and the Strait of Hormuz blockade, which have driven up international crude oil prices significantly.

items.title: IndiGo flight from Delhi to Shirdi diverted to Navi Mumbai due to bad weather
items.summary: An IndiGo flight from Delhi to Shirdi was diverted to Navi Mumbai due to bad weather at the destination, prioritizing passenger safety. This diversion occurred as heavy rainfall was anticipated across Mumbai, prompting IndiGo to issue a travel advisory for passengers to plan journeys in advance and monitor updates.

items.title: India fuel prices rise for third time this month as petrol, diesel get costlier again
items.summary: Petrol and diesel prices were hiked by ₹0.87 and ₹0.91 per litre respectively amid Iran-linked crude oil pressure.

items.title: SpaceX launches Starship test flight on second try
items.summary: SpaceX launched its massive Starship rocket on Friday, a day after the company scrubbed plans before takeoff.

items.title: Petrol, diesel prices hiked for third time in eight days on rising crude oil costs
items.summary: Fuel prices in India have seen another increase, with petrol and diesel rates rising by approximately Rs 0.87 and Rs 0.91 per litre respectively in Delhi. This marks the third hike this month, driven by a surge in international crude oil prices due to Middle East conflict and the expiry of a US sanctions waiver on Russian oil.

items.title: El Nino threat: Centre rolls out kharif contingency plan to shield crops
items.summary: Agriculture ministry has directed states to prepare district-wise crop strategies, and promote short-duration crops such as pulses, certain millets and vegetables during the kharif season because they require less water, mature quickly and help mitigate the vagaries of weather, said officials.

items.title: India-Russia energy ties widen beyond crude to gas, nuclear and petrochemicals
items.summary: India and Russia are deepening energy ties. Discussions focus on gas supplies and joint ventures for hydrocarbon development. Russia is a key crude oil supplier, with US sanctions waivers continuing. The nations are also exploring LPG purchases and new oil and gas facilities. Civil nuclear cooperation is gaining momentum, with projects like Kudankulam showcasing Russian technology.

items.title: Disney's 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' tallies lowest Thursday preview sales in franchise history
items.summary: Disney's "Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu" tallied $12 million in Thursday night previews, the lowest collection of advance tickets in franchise history.

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigning as Trump's intelligence chief
items.summary: Gabbard said she is leaving her post to support her husband, who is battling a rare form of bone cancer.

items.title: UK sees hottest day of year as bank holiday travellers face queues
items.summary: Temperatures hit 28.4C in parts of England on Friday, while delays have been reported at the Port of Dover and Birmingham Airport ahead of the bank holiday weekend.

items.title: From decades to years - AI could speed search for brain drugs hiding in plain sight
items.summary: Researchers hope the work will help identify affordable, effective drugs to treat conditions like MND.

items.title: New lawsuits against Trump's DOJ 'lawfare' fund
items.summary: President Trump droppped his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS in exchange for the DOJ creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate alleged "lawfare" victims.

items.title: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed chair as Trump seeks interest rate cuts
items.summary: Despite President Donald Trump's demands for lower interest rates, markets are betting the Fed will stay on hold through most, if not all, of 2026.

items.title: Qualcomm's stock pop shows investors are 'waking up' to boom in AI devices
items.summary: Qualcomm is on a big run, powered by investor bullishness that the company will be at the center of a boom in AI devices.

items.title: Legal fight could delay a proposed $7B settlement for lawsuits in Roundup cancer claims
items.summary: Legal fight could delay a proposed $7B settlement for lawsuits in Roundup cancer claims

items.title: Treasury yields are mixed as investors digest Fed chair swearing in, bond market volatility
items.summary: The volatility in the bond market continued as new Fed chair Kevin Warsh was sworn in on Friday.

items.title: US stocks today: Dow hits record high as Middle East hopes lift sentiment and Warsh takes Fed charge
items.summary: US stocks rose as Dow hit an intraday record and S&P 500 extended its winning streak to eight weeks, supported by strong earnings and hopes of easing Middle East tensions. Tech stocks gained after Lenovo results lifted Dell and HP. Bond yields eased while Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Fed chair.

items.title: Colgate-Palmolive India leans on premium products, advertising to drive growth
items.summary: Colgate-Palmolive India is boosting investments in premium products and advertising. The company prioritizes volume-driven growth over margin expansion. This strategy aims to capture rising consumer demand for higher-value oral care. Urban demand is recovering, benefiting premium offerings. E-commerce and quick commerce are significant growth drivers, especially for premium products.

items.title: Gold Drops as Fed’s Waller Says Next Move Likely to be Rate Hike
items.summary: Gold declined as traders ramped up bets on Federal Reserve monetary tightening after Governor Christopher Waller warned the Iran war’s energy shock could fuel inflation.

items.title: Hindalco's India segment shines as profit and revenue hit record highs
items.summary: Hindalco Industries achieved its highest ever revenue and profit for India operations in the March quarter. The company's India business delivered outstanding results across aluminium and copper segments. Consolidated revenue and Ebitda also reached all-time highs for the fiscal year. Novelis, the global arm, faced challenges but is on track for plant restarts.

items.title: Emirates NBD to launch open offer for 26% stake in RBL Bank from June 1
items.summary: The offer will open on June 1 and close on June 12.

items.title: Home Depot comps finally catch Lowe's, opening the door for the stock to do the same
items.summary: It took nearly a year. But it finally happened this quarter.

items.title: Singapore, US, UK lead in foreign cos entering India
items.summary: Foreign companies are increasingly setting up shop in India. Singapore, the US, the UK, South Korea, and Germany are leading this trend. Registrations reached a nine-year peak in the last fiscal year. This surge highlights growing international business interest in the Indian market. New companies from South Africa, Ghana, and Uzbekistan also entered India for the first time.

items.title: Dalmia Bharat buys Jaiprakash cement assets from Adani for Rs 2,850 crore
items.summary: Dalmia Bharat is acquiring Jaiprakash Associates' cement business from Adani Group for ₹2,850 crore. This deal adds 5.2 million tonnes of cement capacity. The company is also expanding existing facilities. By FY28, Dalmia Bharat's total production capacity will reach 66.7 million tonnes. The transaction is expected to complete soon. This acquisition strengthens Dalmia Bharat's position as a pan-India player.

items.title: LPG sales dip 13% in April as Hormuz closure chokes supplies
items.summary: LPG supplies saw a sharp drop in April. Households and businesses received less fuel. Industries faced an 82% reduction, prompting a shift to alternatives. Vehicle fuel supplies increased. Overall LPG sales fell 13%, with northern India experiencing the steepest decline. This situation arose from disruptions in fuel imports.

items.title: RBI greenlights record surplus transfer of Rs 2.86 lakh crore
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's central board approved a record Rs 2.86 lakh crore surplus transfer to the government for FY26. This significant boost to non-tax revenues aligns with expectations. The RBI's balance sheet expanded, and net income rose, supported by increased gross income.

items.title: Honda to drive in over 10 new models by 2030
items.summary: Honda is set to introduce more than ten new models in India. This includes sports utility vehicles with petrol, hybrid, and electric powertrains. The company will launch its first electric vehicle in the latter half of the current financial year. Compact and mid-sized SUVs are also planned, with local manufacturing. India is a key market for Honda's future growth.

items.title: High Court says no to 'coercive action' against IndiGo
items.summary: The Delhi High Court has stopped the GST department from taking action against IndiGo's parent company. This is regarding a ₹458 crore GST demand on compensation received from foreign suppliers. The court noted the amount received was compensation, not a taxable supply. The company's counsel assured solvency. The case will be heard again in August.

items.title: PSBs told to draw up plan to boost rural and informal sector lending
items.summary: Government directs state-run banks to identify successful initiatives for wider adoption. The focus will be on lending to unorganised sectors, marginalised groups, and rural populations. This strategy aims to prevent credit crunches for small borrowers and MSMEs. Discussions will also cover IT updates, loan digitalisation, and risk management. The government seeks to boost credit availability for eligible businesses.

items.title: Sun Pharma sees steady FY27 growth, progresses $11.75 bn Organon integration deal
items.summary: Sun Pharma anticipates strong growth in the coming years. The company expects a high single-digit increase in its overall sales by FY2027. Significant progress is being made on the acquisition of Organon, with completion targeted for the last quarter of FY2027. This strategic move is set to enhance Sun Pharma's global presence and product portfolio across key therapeutic areas.

items.title: India blocks China's request for solar, IT dispute panel at WTO
items.summary: India has blocked China's initial request for a World Trade Organization dispute panel. China alleges India's tariffs and incentives discriminate against its IT and solar energy imports. India maintains its measures align with global trade rules. This dispute stems from China's filing last year. China can renew its request at a future meeting.

items.title: OnePlus, Xiaomi among firms trimming marketing and sales roles in India amid cost pressure
items.summary: Smartphone companies are reducing staff in marketing and sales. Rising costs and slower sales are pushing brands to cut expenses and focus on profits. This is impacting corporate and commercial roles. Some companies are eliminating entire teams. This shift reflects a market correction. Employees are taking on broader responsibilities. Salary hikes for new hires have also decreased.

items.title: SpaceX’s Money-Losing Rockets Are Biggest Asset in AI Dream
items.summary: SpaceX revealed eye-popping numbers in its IPO prospectus, including a $26.5 trillion potential market for an empire spanning artificial intelligence and telecommunications.

items.title: Dollar near six-week high amid Iran war jitters
items.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX/ (UPDATE 5):FOREX-Dollar near six-week high amid Iran war jitters

items.title: Cable TV operators win interim relief as Kerala HC pauses TRP overhaul rule
items.summary: The Kerala High Court has put a hold on a new government rule. This rule excluded viewership from default channels, known as landing pages, from TV ratings. Cable operators are concerned about losing revenue. The court's interim order provides relief. The matter will be heard again soon. This policy change affects how TV channels are ranked and viewed.

items.title: Wall Street rises, Dow hits record high as Middle East hopes lift sentiment
items.summary: USA-STOCKS/ (UPDATE 5):US STOCKS-Wall Street rises, Dow hits record high as Middle East hopes lift sentiment

items.title: Fortis liability case: Delhi HC says blame can't be shifted to third party
items.summary: Amid ongoing legal disputes, the Delhi High Court is probing Fortis Healthcare's oversight concerning the sale of shares belonging to the Singh brothers, former promoters of the company. The court's remarks suggest that Fortis had a responsibility to inhibit such transactions.

items.title: SEC Delays Plan Allowing for Crypto Versions of US Stocks
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Commission is delaying a plan to provide broad exemptions for US crypto firms to trade tokenized assets linked to stocks, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Bank boss sorry after describing workers as 'lower value human capital'
items.summary: The Standard Chartered boss said he values all colleagues and is committed to helping them cope with change.

items.title: Wingtech seeks $1.2 billion damages in new suit against Nexperia
items.summary: WINGTECH-CHINA-NEXPERIA:Wingtech seeks $1.2 billion damages in new suit against Nexperia

items.title: RBI MPC: Bank economists back a rate hike in H2 of FY27
items.summary: Bank economists have advised the Reserve Bank of India that interest rate increases will be necessary later this financial year. This is to manage the effects of the West Asia conflict and ensure economic stability. Most economists do not expect a rate hike in the upcoming June policy meeting.

items.title: Timely payments by state discoms aiding power gencos: Moody's
items.summary: Electricity distribution companies are showing better payment habits. This is helping power generators receive payments on time. Key operational metrics have improved significantly. The gap between costs and revenue has narrowed. Technical and commercial losses have also fallen. The sector reported its first profit in years. However, performance varies across states.

items.title: Kevin Warsh's real Fed 'regime change' may happen deep inside Wall Street's plumbing
items.summary: Warsh could guide to a smaller role in day-to-day markets, while also setting clearer rules for how and when it should intervene.

items.title: Trump wants new Fed chair to be 'totally independent'
items.summary: The US president piled major pressure on Kevin Warsh's predecessor to cut interest rates.

items.title: IPL gets 70% of viewership in hybrid mode: Study
items.summary: The findings suggest IPL viewing is increasingly resembling a daily habit rather than event-based consumption. The shift extends beyond live matches. Around 8.4% of respondents said they watched match highlights on YouTube, while 13.64% consumed IPL clips and reels on social media, underscoring how short-form and catch-up formats are extending fan engagement beyond live telecasts.

items.title: RBI approves ICICI Bank CEO Sandeep Bakhshi’s reappointment till 2028
items.summary: Sandeep Bakhshi will continue as ICICI Bank's chief executive for another two years. The Reserve Bank of India has approved his reappointment. Bakhshi's term will now extend until October 3, 2028. He has been leading India's second-largest private lender since 2018. The bank recently reported strong net profits for the quarter ending March 31.

items.title: Tesla to establish sales and service centre in Hyderabad
items.summary: Electric vehicle maker Tesla is preparing to open a sales and service centre in Hyderabad. Representatives met with Telangana's IT Minister D Sridhar Babu. The state government highlighted its strong ecosystem for electric vehicles and advanced technologies. This move signifies Tesla's expansion plans in India. The minister invited Tesla to explore further investment opportunities in Telangana.

items.title: Sebi bars 7 entities over stock manipulation, freezes ₹20 crore gains
items.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has accused seven entities of engaging in stock manipulation through social media, earning over ₹20 crore by enticing retail investors into buying select stocks before selling their holdings at a profit.

items.title: India in better position to manage retail inflation, RBI must hold rates: ASSOCHAM
items.summary: India is well-positioned to handle retail inflation, according to ASSOCHAM. The industry lobby recommends the Reserve Bank of India maintain the current repo rate. Support measures for export-oriented and energy-intensive MSMEs are also advised. India's inflation performance is noted as better than the US. The central bank's recent liquidity injection is appreciated.

items.title: Sun Pharma guides for slower FY27 growth amid regulatory, macro challenges
items.summary: Sun Pharma forecasts high single-digit revenue growth for FY27 after an 11.9% increase in FY26 revenue. The company aims to expand its innovative portfolio amid regulatory challenges and compliance issues, while also focusing on acquisitions and new product launches in the obesity-drug market.

items.title: Central Bank OFS subscribed 2.35 times on Day-1, govt exercises green shoe option
items.summary: The strong response to the OFS comes at a time when the government has set an ambitious disinvestment and asset monetization target of ₹80,000 crore for FY27, nearly 135% higher than the revised divestment estimate of ₹33,837 crore for FY26.

items.title: US Fed chair Kevin Warsh says will be 'reform-oriented' at glitzy White House swearing-in
items.summary: New US Federal Reserve chair Kevin Warsh vowed to be "reform-oriented" as he was sworn in at the White House on Friday, with President Donald Trump insisting the central bank chief would be "totally independent." It is unusual for the chief of the Fed -- an independent non-partisan body that sets monetary policy according to a dual mandate on inflation and employment -- to be sworn in at the White House.

items.title: Small-cap stock hits upper circuit after Q4 results 2026
items.summary: HRS Aluglaze shares rose 6% to ₹239 after reporting strong financial results for FY26, with revenue of ₹67.53 crore, up 60.36%. EBITDA grew 70.02% to ₹18.26 crore, and profit after tax increased 98.56% to ₹10.21 crore, driven by robust project execution.

items.title: The risks to the legacy of Barney Frank
